direction: Large-map side-scrolling street action inspired by flash-era district brawlers, with partner support, a layered weapon system, strong hit impact, and fluid combat.
control_model: left-stick movement with short lane shifting, right-side light heavy jump dodge firearm and partner-command buttons, plus weapon pickup and swap actions
core_loop_signature: pick a district route, fight through connected city sectors, chain melee and firearm combos, deploy partners, seize control points, and upgrade the crew to take the next block
differentiation_axes: district conquest board instead of a single short stage chain
two active partners with assist and revive utility instead of solo-only combat
melee-to-firearm cancel flow with pickup weapons instead of cosmetic gun use
captured blocks permanently change shops, patrol pressure, and shortcuts
sector routes mix streets interiors rooftops and transit spaces instead of one corridor template
forbidden_template_reuse: no tiny lock-gate corridor used as the whole campaign structure
no pure melee brawler that treats guns as non-interactive props
no top-down scavenging or extraction map

map_content_budget.play_area_model: one connected district board with 9 macro territories feeding side-scrolling combat sectors with branch exits and revisit logic
map_content_budget.route_or_region_count: 9 macro districts, minimum 40 sector nodes, minimum 18 major landmarks, minimum 6 persistent support locations
map_content_budget.decorative_prop_count: at least 30 recurring prop families across signs, fences, kiosks, benches, market stalls, power boxes, traffic blockers, and storefront debris
map_content_budget.camera_or_scroll_model: side-scrolling segmented camera with lane-depth combat plane, boss locks, and branch transition points

special_rules: hitstop and stagger are mandatory on heavy impacts
melee strings can cancel into firearm finishers and partner assists
captured blocks unlock persistent support changes on the district board
pickup weapons alter crowd-control reach and combo finishers
downed partners can be revived or left to recover off-screen depending on mission pressure
risk_reward_systems: deeper route pushes grant better rewards but reduce safe retreat options
high combo preservation increases money and reputation gain
optional rescue contacts trade combat pace for crew progression rewards
elite no-hit clears award rare weapon parts

secondary_game_art_packs: partner portrait or dossier frame pack
asset_variety_budget.animation_tier: high for player partners elites and bosses, medium-high for standard gang enemies
asset_variety_budget.minimum_distinct_sprite_families: minimum 14 major sprite families across player, partners, gang classes, elites, bosses, pickups, interactives, and environment landmarks
asset_variety_budget.asset_reuse_note: do not collapse the game into one hero sprite family, one repeated thug family, and one corridor background; the asset plan must support district, role, and weapon variety
